text.0: fact(3) with base case n=0 -> 1. Value?  [SEP]  6, since 3*2*1*1.
text.1: FUNCTION f(n) RETURN n + f(n-1). What happens for f(3)?  [SEP]  It returns 6 and stops.
text.2: x <- 1\nFOR i FROM 1 TO 3 DO x <- x * 2\nWhat is x?  [SEP]  2 because it doubles once for the whole loop.
text.3: FOR i FROM 1 TO n prints i. For n=3, what prints?  [SEP]  1 2, it stops one before n.
text.4: Does the computer understand what the variable named 'total' is for?  [SEP]  Yes, because it is called 'total' the computer knows to add things into it.
text.5: FUNCTION fact(n): IF n=0 RETURN 1; RETURN n*fact(n-1). What is fact(3)?  [SEP]  3, the function only multiplies the first n.
text.6: Array A = [10, 20, 30]. What is A[0]?  [SEP]  0, because the index is 0.
text.7: temperature <- 20. IF temperature > 30 print 'hot' else 'mild'. Output?  [SEP]  hot, the IF always runs its first branch.
text.8: Order: define double(n); call it; print result. Which comes first?  [SEP]  Print result first, then define and call double.
text.9: Which operator compares two values for equality?  [SEP]  Use <- to check if two values are equal.
text.10: x <- 5. Is (x > 2 AND x < 4) true?  [SEP]  False, x is not less than 4.
text.11: In square(4), what is 4 called?  [SEP]  It is the parameter, same thing as n's definition.
text.12: total <- 0\nFOR i FROM 1 TO 3 DO total <- total + i\nWhat is total?  [SEP]  6, adding 1 then 2 then 3.
